# Review: deepmind/1158.lean — Erdős Problem 1158

## 1. Copyright Header
- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement
- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` (line 17).
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring
- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–28).
- [x] Starts with heading: `# Erdős Problem 1158`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `[erdosproblems.com/1158](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1158)`.
- [ ] **Missing brief plain-English description of the problem.** The module docstring contains only the title, reference link, and bibliography entries (`[Er64f]`, `[Va99]`). The actual problem description appears in the theorem docstring (lines 46–58) but the checklist requires it in the module docstring as well.
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Er64f]` and `[Va99]`.

## 4. Namespace
- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1158 ... end Erdos1158` (lines 30–71).
- [x] Namespace name uses UpperCamelCase: `Erdos1158`.
- [x] No `open` statements needed; none present.

## 5. Custom Definitions
- [x] `IsUniformHypergraph` has a LaTeX docstring (line 32).
- [x] `HasKtrCopy` has a LaTeX docstring (lines 36–38).
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed — both definitions return `Prop`, which requires no computation.
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] `IsUniformHypergraph` uses UpperCamelCase (Prop-valued def starting with `Is`).
- [x] `HasKtrCopy` uses UpperCamelCase (Prop-valued def starting with `Has`).
- [x] No `local notation` needed; none used.
- [x] No `@[category test/API]` sanity checks, but this is optional ("consider adding").

## 6. Theorem Statements
- [x] Uses `theorem` keyword (line 61).
- [x] Named `erdos_1158` — snake_case matching namespace number.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]` (line 60).
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 46–58).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Va99, 3.65]` and `[Er64f]`.

### `answer()` usage
The problem asks "Is it true that ex_t(n, K_t(r)) ≥ n^{t − r^{1−t} − o(1)}?" — a yes/no question. This is **Case 1**.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` — correct for a yes/no question.
- [x] `answer(sorry)` is unsolved — correct for an open problem.
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: for all t ≥ 2, r ≥ 2, ε > 0, for sufficiently large n, there exists a t-uniform hypergraph with no K_t(r) copy and at least n^{t − r^{1−t} − ε} edges.
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters — all universals (t, r, ε, n) and existentials (N₀, E) are on the RHS of `↔`.

## 7. Category Attribute
- [x] Exactly one `@[category research open]`.
- [x] Valid category value.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute
- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS 5]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (5 = Combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] 5 is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies
- [x] Uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` format (lines 68–69) — acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
- [x] No variants present. The file formalizes a single conjecture, which is appropriate.

## 11. Code Quality
- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `≤`, `≥`, `¬`, `↔`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `ε`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification
- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**Overall: PASS with one minor docstring issue.**

| # | Section | Verdict |

| 1 | Copyright Header | ✅ Pass |
| 2 | Import Statement | ✅ Pass |
| 3 | Module Docstring | ⚠️ Missing plain-English problem description |

| 11 | Code Quality | ✅ Pass |
| 12 | Build Verification | ⬜ Not tested |

**Suggested fix:** Add a one- or two-sentence plain-English summary of the problem to the module docstring (e.g., "Erdős asks whether the extremal number for complete t-partite t-uniform hypergraphs satisfies ex_t(n, K_t(r)) ≥ n^{t − r^{1−t} − o(1)}."). The theorem docstring already contains an excellent description; a brief version should also appear in the module docstring per §3 of the checklist.

    32→/-- A $t$-uniform hypergraph on $\operatorname{Fin}(n)$: every edge has exactly $t$ vertices. -/

    34→  ∀ e ∈ E, e.card = t
    35→
    36→/-- A $t$-uniform hypergraph $E$ on $\operatorname{Fin}(n)$ contains a copy of the complete
    37→$t$-partite $t$-uniform hypergraph $K_t(r)$ if there exist $t$ pairwise disjoint vertex classes,
    38→each of size $r$, such that every transversal forms an edge of $E$. -/
    39→def HasKtrCopy (t r : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (E : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    40→  ∃ classes : Fin t → Finset (Fin n),
    41→    (∀ i, (classes i).card = r) ∧
    42→    (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (classes i) (classes j)) ∧
    43→    (∀ f : Fin t → Fin n, (∀ i, f i ∈ classes i) →
    44→      Finset.image f Finset.univ ∈ E)
    45→

    47→**Erdős Problem 1158** [Va99, 3.65]:

    49→Let $K_t(r)$ be the complete $t$-partite $t$-uniform hypergraph with $r$ vertices in

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 1158

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1158](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1158)

[Er64f] Erdős, P., _On extremal problems of graphs and generalized graphs_. Israel J. Math. 2
(1964), 183-190.

[Va99] Vu, V. H., _Extremal set systems_. Handbook of Combinatorics (1999), 3.65.
-/

namespace Erdos1158

/-- A $t$-uniform hypergraph on $\operatorname{Fin}(n)$: every edge has exactly $t$ vertices. -/
def IsUniformHypergraph (t : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (E : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
  ∀ e ∈ E, e.card = t

/-- A $t$-uniform hypergraph $E$ on $\operatorname{Fin}(n)$ contains a copy of the complete
$t$-partite $t$-uniform hypergraph $K_t(r)$ if there exist $t$ pairwise disjoint vertex classes,
each of size $r$, such that every transversal forms an edge of $E$. -/
def HasKtrCopy (t r : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (E : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
  ∃ classes : Fin t → Finset (Fin n),
    (∀ i, (classes i).card = r) ∧
    (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (classes i) (classes j)) ∧
    (∀ f : Fin t → Fin n, (∀ i, f i ∈ classes i) →
      Finset.image f Finset.univ ∈ E)

/--
**Erdős Problem 1158** [Va99, 3.65]:

Let $K_t(r)$ be the complete $t$-partite $t$-uniform hypergraph with $r$ vertices in
each class. Is it true that $\mathrm{ex}_t(n, K_t(r)) \geq n^{t - r^{1-t} - o(1)}$ for all $t, r$?

Erdős [Er64f] proved that
$n^{t - O(r^{1-t})} \leq \mathrm{ex}_t(n, K_t(r)) \ll n^{t - r^{1-t}}$.
This is only known when $t = 2$ and $2 \leq r \leq 3$. The case $t = 2$ is problem \#714.

Formally: for all $t \geq 2$, $r \geq 2$, and $\varepsilon > 0$, for sufficiently large $n$,
there exists a $t$-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices with no $K_t(r)$ copy and at least
$n^{t - r^{1-t} - \varepsilon}$ edges.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_1158 : answer(sorry) ↔
    ∀ (t r : ℕ), 2 ≤ t → 2 ≤ r →
    ∀ ε : ℝ, 0 < ε →
    ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, N₀ ≤ n →
      ∃ E : Finset (Finset (Fin n)),
        IsUniformHypergraph t E ∧
        ¬HasKtrCopy t r E ∧
        (E.card : ℝ) ≥ (n : ℝ) ^ ((t : ℝ) - (r : ℝ) ^ (1 - (t : ℝ)) - ε) := by
  sorry

end Erdos1158

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 1158

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1158](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1158)

[Er64f] Erdős, P., _On extremal problems of graphs and generalized graphs_. Israel J. Math. 2
(1964), 183-190.

[Va99] Vu, V. H., _Extremal set systems_. Handbook of Combinatorics (1999), 3.65.
-/

namespace Erdos1158

/-- A $t$-uniform hypergraph on $\operatorname{Fin}(n)$: every edge has exactly $t$ vertices. -/
def IsUniformHypergraph (t : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (E : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
  ∀ e ∈ E, e.card = t

/-- A $t$-uniform hypergraph $E$ on $\operatorname{Fin}(n)$ contains a copy of the complete
$t$-partite $t$-uniform hypergraph $K_t(r)$ if there exist $t$ pairwise disjoint vertex classes,
each of size $r$, such that every transversal forms an edge of $E$. -/
def HasKtrCopy (t r : ℕ) {n : ℕ} (E : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
  ∃ classes : Fin t → Finset (Fin n),
    (∀ i, (classes i).card = r) ∧
    (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (classes i) (classes j)) ∧
    (∀ f : Fin t → Fin n, (∀ i, f i ∈ classes i) →
      Finset.image f Finset.univ ∈ E)

/--
**Erdős Problem 1158** [Va99, 3.65]:

Let $K_t(r)$ be the complete $t$-partite $t$-uniform hypergraph with $r$ vertices in
each class. Is it true that $\mathrm{ex}_t(n, K_t(r)) \geq n^{t - r^{1-t} - o(1)}$ for all $t, r$?

Erdős [Er64f] proved that
$n^{t - O(r^{1-t})} \leq \mathrm{ex}_t(n, K_t(r)) \ll n^{t - r^{1-t}}$.
This is only known when $t = 2$ and $2 \leq r \leq 3$. The case $t = 2$ is problem \#714.

Formally: for all $t \geq 2$, $r \geq 2$, and $\varepsilon > 0$, for sufficiently large $n$,
there exists a $t$-uniform hypergraph on $n$ vertices with no $K_t(r)$ copy and at least
$n^{t - r^{1-t} - \varepsilon}$ edges.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_1158 : answer(sorry) ↔
    ∀ (t r : ℕ), 2 ≤ t → 2 ≤ r →
    ∀ ε : ℝ, 0 < ε →
    ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, N₀ ≤ n →
      ∃ E : Finset (Finset (Fin n)),
        IsUniformHypergraph t E ∧
        ¬HasKtrCopy t r E ∧
        (E.card : ℝ) ≥ (n : ℝ) ^ ((t : ℝ) - (r : ℝ) ^ (1 - (t : ℝ)) - ε) := by
  sorry

end Erdos1158
